title: Sorrow
content:
Sorrow like a ceaseless rain
            Beats upon my heart.
        People twist and scream in pain,--
        Dawn will find them still again;
        This has neither wax nor wane,
            Neither stop nor start.

        People dress and go to town;
            I sit in my chair.
        All my thoughts are slow and brown:
        Standing up or sitting down
        Little matters, or what gown
            Or what shoes I wear.
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
